title: Bridge Essay: Spontaneous cognition and its relationship to human creativity: A functional connectivity study involving a chain free association task.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Non-fabrication — spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, creative insight, DMN.
**Phase context:** Spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, creative cognition.

**Spontaneous cognition and its relationship to human creativity: A functional connectivity study involving a chain free association task.** — Neuroimage (2020). DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2020.117064. PMID: 32574810. Resting-state functional connectivity (RSFC) between various brain regions is thought to be associated with creative abilities. Extensive research correlating RSFC with performance on creativity tasks has revealed some of the RSFC patterns characterizing 'the creative brain'. Yet, our understanding of the neurocognitive processes underlying creative thinking still remains limited.
